title: Platform Apps Helm Chart Values Schema
description: Schema for OpenTargets platform Helm chart values

gcpProject.description: The Google Cloud Project ID where the cluster lives.

prefix.description: The prefix for all resource. This should identify the environment (e.g., `dev`, `production`). Keep in mind, for us, `staging` is not an environment itself.

product.description: The product name: either `platform` or `ppp`. This will be appended to the prefix for resource names. It will also affect domain names, resource allocation and some configuration values.

domain.description: The domain name where the application will be exposed

dnsZone.description: The Google Cloud DNS zone name where the domain is managed

production.description: The production environment selector (blue/green). CAUTION: This setting controls which environment is live!

staging.description: Whether the staging environment is enabled or disabled. If disabled, staging services will point to production deployments too.

rateLimiterWhitelist.description: List of IPs to allow through the API rate limiter (in CIDR notation).

blacklist.description: List of IPs to block from accessing the API (in CIDR notation).

googleTagManagerId.description: The Google Tag Manager ID to set in the production Web Application. It should have the format `GTM-XXXXXX`.

blue.description: The config for the blue deployment

green.description: The config for the green deployment

disk.description: The disk configuration for database storage classes

clickhouse.description: The ClickHouse infrastructure sizing, shared across all colors

opensearch.description: The OpenSearch infrastructure sizing, shared across all colors

deployment.description: The deployment configuration for a color (blue/green), which will be mapped to an environment (production/staging)

clickhouse.description: The ClickHouse database configuration for this deployment

opensearch.description: The OpenSearch database configuration for this deployment

iops.description: Provisioned IOPS for the hyperdisk storage class

throughput.description: Provisioned throughput for the hyperdisk storage class (e.g., 140Mi)

snapshot.description: The GCP snapshot name to restore ClickHouse data from
The GCP snapshot name to restore OpenSearch data from

clickhouseResources.description: ClickHouse infrastructure sizing (shared across all colors)

opensearchResources.description: OpenSearch infrastructure sizing (shared across all colors)

webappService.description: The config for the WebApp service

apiService.description: The config for the API service

aiapiService.description: The config for the AI API service

secrets.description: The secrets for the AI API service

openai.description: The OpenAI token secret name. This secret must exist in the same project as the GKE cluster.

imageConfig.description: The full container image reference including tag (e.g., `gcr.io/open-targets-dev/platform/webapp:25.2.2`)

serviceResources.description: The resource allocation for a deployment

minReplicas.description:
The minimum number of replicas for the deployment.
Things to keep in mind:
 * Do not set to 0 for disabling an environment; use the `staging` root property instead.
 * This should be at least 2 for production to allow for rolling updates.

maxReplicas.description: The maximum number of replicas for the deployment.

requests.description: The resource requests for the deployment. These values decide the scheduling of pods.

limits.description: The resource limits for the deployment. These values decide the maximum resources a pod can use.

memory.description: The memory requirement (e.g., 128Mi, 1Gi). A node with at least this available memory will be a candidate for scheduling the pod.

cpu.description: The CPU requirement (e.g., 100m, 1000m, 2). A node with at least this available CPU will be a candidate for scheduling the pod.

memory.description: The memory limit (e.g., 256Mi, 2Gi). The pod will be oom-killed if it exceeds this limit.

cpu.description: The CPU limit (e.g., 200m, 2000m, 4). The pod will be throttled if it exceeds this limit.
